text.title: Exploring Tehran - Top Things to do & Tips (Inside Iran, Episode 01)
text.u: [Music]
the Islamic Republic of Iran also known
as Persia is located in Western Asia and
is home to one of the world's oldest
civilizations Iran is also the second
largest country in the Middle East
bordered by Iraq Armenia turkistan
Afghanistan and Pakistan and with about
83 million inhabitants the 17 most
populous country in the world
this documentary series aims to
introduce you to the most popular and
lesson known things to do in Iran as
well as the Persian Cuisine a bit of
history and the super friendly locals me
and my buddy David met on our more than
3 week lasting trip starting with the
capital Tan in this episode welcome to
inside Iran
[Music]
[Applause]
Salam from Iran me and my buddy David we
just landed here in tran and we will be
discovering Iran in the next few weeks
together where are we now this is the
assadi monument it's one of the most
iconic buildings here in tran so let's
dive in and explore Tran
[Music]
together Tran is the capital of the
country country with a population of
about 15 million in the metropolitan
area and with that also the country's
largest city as well as its leading
economic and Cultural
Center the BOS asadi meaning Freedom
Tower is one of the symbols of tan and
marks the West entrance to the city it
was commissioned by Muhammad Raa palavi
the last Sha of Iran to Mark 2,500 years
of the Persian Empire
I love the symmetry of that
thing in tan the easiest way to get
around is taking the metro have a pretty
good system here and it's just about 10
cents 10
cents another must thing in tran is the
gastan palace which literally means the
Palace of flowers because of the
beautiful gardens you have inside and it
is also the former Royal kajar complex
the kajar dynasty was an Iranian Royal
dynasty that followed the San Dynasty
and ruled Persia from
1785 till
1925 the gastan palace is one of the
oldest historic monuments in the city of
Tran and belongs to a group of Royal
buildings that were once inclosed within
the mud thatched walls of Tran Citadel
it consists of Gardens Royal buildings
and collections of Iranian crafts and
European presents from the 18th and 19th
centuries for some it might be
surprising how modern Iran and
especially Tran is a good example for
that is the sheer amount of coffee shops
you can find really hip places so I want
to show you around a bit and show you my
favorite coffee shops in town and I
start this one with e want coffee yeah
exactly
[Music]
[Applause]
[Music]
a good spot to go to on Fridays is the
30 tier Street where you can find all
these van cafes and food places
[Music]
so it's time to Feast typical Iranian we
went to sakuna a very typical Iranian
place and we've ordered the national
dish which is Kabab and
dizzy which is kind of a thick
soup another indicator that Iran and
Tran is so modern is that they also have
apps like we have for example but here
it is called Snap It Works basically the
same way and today my driver is Armin
Armin is taking me to the other side of
town our friend gazal from Tran took us
up here to the roof of tan where you can
enjoy a beautiful view of the city and
this is what we're going to do now Mount
toal is located right next to Tran
making it a popular recreational region
for its residents the bami Tran is a
viewpoint located near the bottom
station of the gondola lift and a great
spot to see the scale of the
Metropolis that's another cool thing you
can do in tran is Rise down the toal on
this
track as Iran is located on the Silk
Road the trading business always played
a major role till to date in Iran this
is why you should visit the big Bazar
here in tran which is a true Maze of
more than 11 kilm of
corridors so David is thinking about
buying a carpet because we mightbe want
to fly to isan tomorrow that looks
nice when he visits the grand Bazar you
also needs to buy something so we got
some nuts for
us
good another great Bazar to visit here
inan is the tarish Bazar which is
located in one of the oldest
neighborhoods in
[Music]
[Applause]
trra we now in front of the former US
Embassy and if you know a little bit of
history behind it you know there's no
Embassy in tan anymore instead you have
a lot of very interesting murals here
because Iran and the US don't have
historically the best
relationship so which used to be the US
Embassy is now kind of a weird
Museum not far away from the US Embassy
there's this beautiful Park and I'm here
with my local friends Mona and Maria hi
where are we we are in an artist Park
and it's popular because of the House of
artist and cafes as
well got a a local recommendation for
restaurant here called Ada restaurant
which is close to the Sadi Metro and we
have today uh one dish we were looking
for for like a week now which is called
tachin which is basically rice it is
baked and we got with that eggplant and
chicken really tender tender
juicy very nice fish highly
recommended another popular tourist
destination you should consider visiting
is the 300 Hector sadabad complex in the
north of Tran which was built in the
19th century by the kajar and palaven
monarchs and basically served as their
summer
residents After the Revolution it got
turned into a museum so nowadays it
encompasses palaces museums but also a
180 hect big natural Forest so it's a
great getaway from the hustling and
buzzling City Center of
[Music]
Tran we now took a snap to get to the
six largest tower in the world called
Milad Tower and I guess you have the
best view over tan from up there so
let's check out the
view the bosim Milad also known as the
Tran Tower is also the 17th tallest
freestanding structure in the world
standing at 435 M from base to the tip
of the antenna the tower is a part of a
complex called international trade and
Convention Center of Tran the complex
also includes a five-star hotel a
Convention Center a World Trade Center
and an IT
Park even so tan is a quite busy City
with more than 50 million inhabitants
you can find green spots like in nestan
gardens throughout the city which you
can use to unwind and relax
so the tour of hidden gems continues
with the Masuda Palace which happens to
be just around the corner from the
nestan garden and is one of the most
beautiful palaces of the Kaja
[Music]
Dynasty as Tran is always full of
surprises I stumbled upon this Square
here it's called the mushk square on the
way to the next spot I want to take you
this Square was also built during Kaja
dasty and interesting enough at the time
it was built it was outside of the city
limits it was north of Tran pretty
impressive actually see you have a like
you have this gate here for example you
have these massive buildings
here and now it's right in the city
center and the next hit gem I'm going to
show you is the mam Museum which is
considered to be one of the most
beautiful houses here in the center of
tan and it is also from the Kaja
period so if you're wondering about
nightlife in tran actually um there are
two options you don't have night life or
you have friends in tan around tan and
they invite you to their home and then
like we today we have a little relax
barbecue with friends and tea of
course and nice chess and this is how
you get to know the culture in IR which
is really
recomendable in the next episode we'll
show you one of the largest squares in
the world visit some impressive mosques
and introduce you to even more delicious
Persian food make sure to subscribe to
keep following the series and check out
the related Iran travel guide where you
can find all information needed
including the mentioned spots and
restaurants for your trip to Iran
[Music]
a
[Applause]
[Music]
